article_meta.title: Iranians block a street during a protest in Tehran, Iran on 9 January 2026

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Word Choice","biasedText":"Iranâs brutal crackdown on anti-regime protests","explanation":"The author uses the word 'brutal' as their own characterization of Iran's crackdown, which is an editorializing adjective in news copy rather than a neutral descriptor. Similarly, 'anti-regime' is a loaded framing choice."},{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"Doctors are being arrested in Iran for helping save the lives of some of the tens of thousands injured","explanation":"The author frames the arrests as being specifically 'for helping save the lives,' which presupposes the government's motive and presents only one interpretation of why the arrests occurred, rather than neutrally reporting that doctors who treated protesters were arrested."},{"biasType":"Bias by Omission","biasedText":"Iranian authorities have not publicly commented on Golchiniâs detention, nor have they confirmed any charges against him.","explanation":"While the article acknowledges the lack of official comment, there is no Iranian government perspective presented anywhere in the article explaining or justifying the arrests from their point of view. The only Iranian official quoted is the judiciary chief urging no leniency, which is used to reinforce the article's framing rather than provide an alternative perspective."},{"biasType":"Subjective Qualifying Adjectives","biasedText":"in an extremely brutal manner and was severely beaten","explanation":"While this phrase is attributed to a source via IHRNGO, the author's own framing earlier uses similarly loaded language like 'brutal crackdown' without attribution, establishing a pattern of editorializing adjectives."},{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"shot or stabbed at close range, and in some cases set up makeshift treatment centres","explanation":"The author includes graphic details ('shot or stabbed at close range') in their own connecting prose to evoke emotional response, without attributing these specific claims to a named source."}]}

detections.biasedText: Iranâs brutal crackdown on anti-regime protests
detections.explanation: The author uses the word 'brutal' as their own characterization of Iran's crackdown, which is an editorializing adjective in news copy rather than a neutral descriptor. Similarly, 'anti-regime' is a loaded framing choice.

detections.biasedText: Doctors are being arrested in Iran for helping save the lives of some of the tens of thousands injured
detections.explanation: The author frames the arrests as being specifically 'for helping save the lives,' which presupposes the government's motive and presents only one interpretation of why the arrests occurred, rather than neutrally reporting that doctors who treated protesters were arrested.

detections.biasedText: Iranian authorities have not publicly commented on Golchiniâs detention, nor have they confirmed any charges against him.
detections.explanation: While the article acknowledges the lack of official comment, there is no Iranian government perspective presented anywhere in the article explaining or justifying the arrests from their point of view. The only Iranian official quoted is the judiciary chief urging no leniency, which is used to reinforce the article's framing rather than provide an alternative perspective.

detections.biasedText: in an extremely brutal manner and was severely beaten
detections.explanation: While this phrase is attributed to a source via IHRNGO, the author's own framing earlier uses similarly loaded language like 'brutal crackdown' without attribution, establishing a pattern of editorializing adjectives.

detections.biasedText: shot or stabbed at close range, and in some cases set up makeshift treatment centres
detections.explanation: The author includes graphic details ('shot or stabbed at close range') in their own connecting prose to evoke emotional response, without attributing these specific claims to a named source.
